name: Hario V60
description: The classic Japanese dripper. Produces a clean, bright cup that highlights origin character and delicate florals.
notes: Use a slow, controlled pour and a gooseneck kettle for best results. Works beautifully with light to medium roasts.

name: French Press
description: Full immersion brewing for a rich, heavy-bodied cup. Retains oils and fines for a textured, bold experience.
notes: Skim the crust at 4 minutes before plunging to dramatically reduce bitterness.

name: Moka Pot
description: Italian stovetop brewer that pushes pressurised steam through coffee. Produces a concentrated, espresso-adjacent brew.
notes: Pre-heat the water before filling the bottom chamber to avoid bitter overextraction from slow metal heating.

name: Clever Dripper
description: Combines immersion steeping with a pour-over filter. Forgiving and consistent — great for dialling in new beans.
notes: Adjust steep time (3–5 min) to taste. Very forgiving for new beans before you've fully dialled in.

name: South Indian Filter
description: Traditional stainless steel filter that produces a slow, concentrated decoction. The soul of filter kaapi — best paired with hot milk and sugar.
notes: Use a chicory blend (80:20) for authentic filter kaapi. Never rush — the slow drip is essential to the flavour.

name: 1Zpresso K-Ultra
description: A precision hand grinder with a 48-step external adjustment ring. Exceptionally uniform grind from espresso to coarse filter. Built like a tank.
notes: Each full rotation of the ring = 1 click. Zero point: tighten until burrs touch, then back off to your starting click count.
